issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region – product count badge
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter.applyFilters` function updates the product count badge (`_updateCountBadge`) to reflect the number of visible products, but it does not announce this change to screen‑reader users via an ARIA live region.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region – empty‑state visibility
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter.applyFilters` function shows or hides the empty‑state element (`_updateEmptyState`) when no products match the filter, but it does not provide an ARIA live announcement of this state change.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region – pagination text
issuesFound.explanation: The pagination element (`_paginationEl`) is updated with “Page X of Y” text in `_syncPagination`, but this change is not announced to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region – active filter chips
issuesFound.explanation: The `activeFilterChips.render` function rebuilds the list of active filter “chips” when filters change, but it does not announce the addition or removal of chips to screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region – chip removal
issuesFound.explanation: When a user clicks a filter‑chip’s remove button (`_onChipClick`), the corresponding filter is cleared, but no live‑region announcement is made to inform the user that the filter was removed.
issuesFound.fix:
After removing the chip, announce the change:
    ```js
    liveRegion.announce(`Filter ${label} removed`);
    ```

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region – filter tab selection
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a filter tab (`_onTabClick`) changes the active product category, but the change is not announced to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the active category, announce it:
    ```js
    liveRegion.announce(`Filter category changed to ${value}`);
    ```

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region – sort selection
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the sort order via the sort `<select>` (`_onSortChange`) updates the product list, yet no live‑region announcement informs users of the new sort.
issuesFound.fix:
After applying the new sort, announce it:
    ```js
    liveRegion.announce(`Sorted by ${_productFilterState.sortKey} ${_productFilterState.sortDir}`);
    ```

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region – text search filter
issuesFound.explanation: Typing into the product search field (`_onTextFilterInput`) filters the product list, but there is no live‑region announcement of the number of matching products.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region – view‑mode toggle
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between grid and list view (`_onViewToggle`) updates the layout, but no live‑region announcement is made to convey the new view mode.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling the view, announce it:
    ```js
    liveRegion.announce(`View changed to ${mode} mode`);
    ```

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region – pagination navigation
issuesFound.explanation: When the user navigates to a different page of products via pagination (`goToPage`), the page number changes but no live‑region announcement informs the user of the new page.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating `_productFilterState.page`, add:
    ```js
    liveRegion.announce(`Page ${_productFilterState.page} of ${totalPages}`);
    ```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) controls the mobile drawer (`#nav-drawer`) but the script only adds `aria-controls` if the drawer already has an `id`. The drawer element lacks an `id`, so the button has no `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter sort change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: In `_onSortChange` the sort order is changed and the product grid is refreshed, but no `aria-live` announcement is made to inform screen‑reader users of the new sort.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter pagination change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `goToPage(page)` updates the visible cards and the pagination text, yet there is no `aria-live` update describing the new page.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the pagination text, announce the change:
```js
liveRegion.announce(`Page ${page} of ${totalPages}`);
```

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count badge update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge(count)` changes the badge text but does not provide an `aria-live` notification, leaving users unaware of how many products match the filter.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter empty‑state visibility not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When no products match, `_updateEmptyState` shows the empty‑state element but does not announce the “no results” message.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter category tab buttons lack proper tab semantics
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tabs (`[data-filter-value]`) are toggled with `aria-pressed` but are not marked as a tab list (`role="tablist"`) and the buttons lack `role="tab"` and `aria-selected`, which is required for a true tab interface.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter category tabs missing `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The tab buttons control the product grid but no `aria-controls` attribute points to the grid (`[data-product-grid]`), so screen readers cannot associate the control with the controlled region.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu panels are not announced when opened
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance` adds `aria-expanded="true"` to the trigger and removes `hidden` from the panel, but no live‑region announcement is made, so users may not know a submenu has opened.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons (`[data-period]`) change the displayed prices but do not reference the price container via `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix:
Give the price container an `id` (e.g., `id="price-display"`), then set on each button:
```js
setAttr(btn,'aria-controls','price-display');
```

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle button missing `aria-label`
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons (`[data-view-mode]`) change the grid layout but have no descriptive `aria-label`, making their purpose unclear to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an accessible label:
```js
setAttr(btn,'aria-label', btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode')==='grid' ? 'Grid view' : 'List view');
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” function does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds CSS classes and shows the drawer but never updates aria‑expanded on navToggle, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the navigation is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” function does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open adds the open class and animates the panel, but never sets aria‑expanded on the button that controls the answer, leaving the expanded state invisible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Inline field validator never sets aria‑invalid when a field fails validation
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult calls srUtils.markInvalid which only adds aria‑describedby and an error message; it never adds aria‑invalid="true". Consequently, assistive technologies cannot detect that the field is invalid.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” does not set aria‑expanded to true on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes and shows the drawer, but never updates the aria‑expanded state of the hamburger button. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know that the navigation is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu “open” does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds CSS classes, but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the button that opened it. Assistive technologies therefore think the menu is still collapsed.

issuesFound.title: Search box “open” does not set aria‑expanded to true on the input element
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._open() makes the suggestion list visible but never updates aria‑expanded on the search input, leaving the state ambiguous for screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” does not set aria‑expanded to true on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands the answer panel but never updates the aria‑expanded attribute on the question button, so screen‑reader users cannot detect the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden with CSS only, no aria‑hidden state
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but never adds aria‑hidden="true". The drawer remains in the accessibility tree while invisible, causing focusable elements to be reachable unintentionally.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: _productFilter._updateCountBadge() only changes the text of the badge. No aria‑live region is used, so screen‑reader users are unaware when the number of matching products changes.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu open/close actions are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() and _closeInstance() manage visibility but never provide an audible cue. Users relying on AT may not know when a submenu has opened or closed.

issuesFound.title: Active‑filter chips are rendered without any screen‑reader announcement
issuesFound.explanation: activeFilterChips.render() creates dismissible chips but never notifies users when a filter is added or removed. The change is invisible to AT.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload field does not provide live‑region feedback for upload progress or errors
issuesFound.explanation: fileUploadField updates a <progress> element but never announces status changes (e.g., “upload started”, “50 % uploaded”, “upload failed”). Users of assistive technology receive no feedback.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation toggle button lacks aria‑pressed for its two‑state nature
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button toggles the navigation drawer open and closed, but only uses aria‑expanded. For a button that represents a binary state, aria‑pressed should also be present to convey the pressed/unpressed condition.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes and shows the drawer but never updates the aria‑expanded attribute on the hamburger button. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know when the navigation is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); so the button reflects the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close does not set aria‑hidden on the drawer when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() removes CSS classes and hides the drawer but never marks the drawer with aria‑hidden="true". The element remains in the accessibility tree while invisible, causing confusion for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: After hiding, call setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); (and remove it when opening).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() adds the open class to the panel and removes hidden, but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger element. Users of assistive tech cannot detect the open state.
issuesFound.fix: Inside _openInstance(), add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when the menu opens.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close does not set aria‑hidden on its panel when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance() removes the open class and adds hidden, yet the panel still lacks aria‑hidden="true". The hidden panel stays in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: After adding hidden, also call setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded to true on its question button
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands the answer panel but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users cannot tell which FAQ item is open.
issuesFound.fix: In open(item) add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when expanding.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close does not set aria‑hidden on its answer panel when collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.close() animates the panel closed but does not apply aria‑hidden="true". The collapsed panel remains perceivable to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: After the hide animation, call setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play/pause toggle does not update aria‑pressed on the control button
issuesFound.explanation: testimonialsCarousel.togglePlay() starts and stops auto‑play but only updates aria‑checked on a separate switch; the play/pause button lacks aria‑pressed, so its pressed state is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix: In togglePlay() add setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing)); to reflect the current state.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter subscription form validation does not set aria‑invalid on the email field when invalid
issuesFound.explanation: newsletterForm._onSubmit() validates the email with validateField but never marks the <input type="email"> with aria‑invalid="true" on failure, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the error.
issuesFound.fix: When result.valid is false, call setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and clear it on success.

issuesFound.title: Contact form validation does not set aria‑invalid on fields that fail validation
issuesFound.explanation: contactForm._onSubmit() uses validateField to check inputs but does not apply aria‑invalid to the offending fields, so errors are invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: For each field where result.valid is false, call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and remove it when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close does not announce the closing action to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() hides the drawer but does not provide a live‑region announcement, unlike mobileNav.open() which announces “Navigation menu opened”. Users relying on auditory feedback receive no indication that the menu closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosedAnnouncement || 'Navigation menu closed'); at the end of close().

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions do not announce state changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Neither _openInstance() nor _closeInstance() provide live‑region feedback, so users are not informed when a dropdown menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce(‘Menu opened’); and after closing, liveRegion.announce(‘Menu closed’); or use appropriate strings.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions do not announce state changes via live region (duplicate of 5/6 but for announcements)
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() and close() only announce the question text but not a generic “expanded”/“collapsed” message, which may be insufficient for some users.
issuesFound.fix: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened || ‘Answer expanded’); and liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed || ‘Answer collapsed’); respectively.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions in the dropdown menu controller add or remove CSS classes to show/hide the panel, but they never set or clear the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: FAQ triggers lack initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: During page load the FAQ parser sets `aria-controls` on each trigger but does not set an initial `aria-expanded` value. The default state of the trigger is therefore ambiguous to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
When parsing each FAQ item, initialise the attribute based on the markup’s default state:

```javascript
const isInitiallyOpen = getAttrBool(el, 'data-faq-open') ||
                         getAttrBool(trigger, 'aria-expanded');
setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, String(isInitiallyOpen));
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions change the visibility of the mobile navigation drawer but never set or update `aria-expanded` on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` in `open()` and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` in `close()`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button (`_trigger`) controls the drawer (`_drawer`) but no `aria-controls` attribute is set, so assistive technologies cannot associate the button with the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init()` after obtaining `_trigger` and `_drawer`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-drawer'))`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles between open and closed states (a two‑state toggle) but never updates `aria‑pressed`, violating ARIA best practices for toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true')`; in `close()` set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element (`_drawer`) is shown/hidden by adding/removing CSS classes, but no `aria-hidden` is applied. When hidden, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree, causing confusion for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`; in `close()` add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (mobile navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never updates the aria‑expanded attribute on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') in mobileNav.open and setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') in mobileNav.close.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (dropdown menus)
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick the dropdown panel is shown/hidden by toggling CSS classes, but the button’s aria‑expanded state is never changed.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after closing, call setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (FAQ accordion)
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open adds the CSS.open class and animates the panel but does not set aria‑expanded="true" on the question button, while faqAccordion.close does set it to “false”. Assistive technologies therefore receive inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') before animating.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate marks a field as invalid by calling srUtils.markInvalid, which only adds aria‑describedby and a CSS class. It never sets aria‑invalid="true", so screen readers do not announce the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In the if (!result.valid) branch, add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') before calling srUtils.markInvalid.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, inlineValidator calls srUtils.markValid but does not remove aria‑invalid. The attribute remains, causing persistent error announcements.
issuesFound.fix: In the else branch, add removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid') or setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false') when the field is valid.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content change not announced via live region (FAQ accordion)
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open and close do not write any text to a live‑region after expanding or collapsing an answer. Users of assistive technology receive no audible cue that the panel state changed.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer expanded`); after closing, announce “… answer collapsed”.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden navigation drawer not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close hides the drawer by removing CSS classes and adding is‑hidden but never sets aria‑hidden="true" on the drawer element. The drawer remains perceivable to screen readers even when invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close, add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in mobileNav.open remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden product cards not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: filterAnimation.run hides cards by setting hidden and aria‑hidden only when prefersReducedMotion is true. In the normal animation path the cards are hidden via CSS opacity and class changes without aria‑hidden, leaving them in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: When a card is hidden (in the “toHide” loop), also call setAttr(el, 'aria-hidden', 'true') before starting the fade‑out animation.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown buttons have aria-haspopup but no aria-controls linking them to the panel element. This breaks the relationship for AT.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each trigger has setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panel.id) where panel.id is generated if missing.

issuesFound.title: User action (product filter tab click) does not announce via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a filter tab updates the product grid, but no live‑region is updated to announce the new filter state (“All products shown” etc.). Users of screen readers receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: After applying filters, call liveRegion.announce(`Filter applied: ${categoryLabel}`) or similar.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update `aria-expanded` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`) controls the off‑canvas drawer, but the script never sets or removes `aria-expanded` on that button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open()` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close()` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle lacks `aria-controls` linking to the drawer

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons never update `aria-expanded` when panels open/close
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that open dropdown panels lack dynamic `aria-expanded` updates, breaking the disclosure pattern.

In `_openInstance` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_closeInstance` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels are hidden with `hidden` but never receive `aria-hidden` state updates
issuesFound.explanation: When a panel is closed the script only adds the `hidden` attribute; `aria-hidden` remains unchanged, so AT may still expose the content.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_openInstance` add `inst.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`; in `_closeInstance` add `inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not toggle `aria-expanded` when the suggestion list opens/closes
issuesFound.explanation: The combobox‑style search field never updates `aria-expanded`, so users cannot tell whether suggestions are visible.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_open` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_close` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input lacks `aria-controls` for the suggestion list when the list is generated dynamically
issuesFound.explanation: The listbox is created after the input exists, but the input’s `aria-controls` attribute is only set if the input already has an `id`. If the input has no `id`, the attribute points to a non‑existent element.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the input receives an `id` (e.g., `srUtils.ensureId(_inputEl, 'search-input')`) before calling `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-controls', _listId)`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle switch does not expose `aria-pressed` state
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off switch for annual vs. monthly billing is a `button` that changes visual state but never updates `aria-pressed`, leaving AT users unaware of the current period.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'))`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period buttons (`data-period`) do not update `aria-pressed` when selected
issuesFound.explanation: Each period button changes the pricing but never reflects its selected state via `aria-pressed`.
issuesFound.fix:
After setting the new period, iterate `_periodBtns` and set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-period') === period))`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons never receive `aria-expanded` updates
issuesFound.explanation: The question buttons lack dynamic `aria-expanded`, breaking the disclosure pattern for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels are hidden with `hidden` but never receive `aria-hidden` updates
issuesFound.explanation: When a panel is collapsed the script only adds `hidden`; `aria-hidden` remains unchanged, potentially exposing hidden content to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open` add `item.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`; in `close` add `item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not announce panel state changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a FAQ item does not send a polite/alert live‑region message, so users are not informed of the change.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button lacks `role="switch"` when not a native `button` element
issuesFound.explanation: If the toggle is a `div` or `span`, it does not convey its role to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
If `_toggleEl.tagName !== 'BUTTON'`, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'role', 'switch')` and ensure it is focusable (`tabindex="0"`).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack `role="tab"` and `aria-selected` when used as a tab‑list

issuesFound.title: Pricing savings message updates are not announced via a live region

issuesFound.fix: Line 1148: <Textarea id="contact-message" name="message" rows={6} required aria-required="true" invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error} aria-invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error} aria-describedby={['contact-message-counter', message.state.touched && message.state.error ? 'contact-message-error' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} .../>

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit button missing aria‑busy
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button shows a loading state but does not set aria‑busy, so AT cannot know the button is busy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1034: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" loading={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}> {isSubmitting ? 'Subscribing…' : 'Subscribe'} </Button>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm submit button missing aria‑busy
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form’s submit button enters a loading state without aria‑busy, leaving AT unaware of the busy state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1156: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" loading={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}> {isSubmitting ? 'Sending…' : 'Send message'} </Button>

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation active link missing aria‑current
issuesFound.explanation: The link marked as current via class “nav-link--current” does not have aria‑current="page", so AT cannot identify the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 389: {item.label}

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop missing interactive role
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop <div> is clickable to close the menu but lacks role="button" and tabindex, making it inaccessible to keyboard and AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: <div role="button" tabIndex={0} className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} aria-label="Close navigation menu" />

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm aria-describedby references non‑existent id
issuesFound.explanation: The email input’s aria-describedby points to “newsletter-email-error”, but no element with that id is ever rendered, resulting in a broken reference.
issuesFound.fix:
(fix requires adding an element with id="newsletter-email-error" that contains the error message, e.g.)
  Line 1025: <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} />
